gto.ae.quests.0BC456B4331C09F5.descripti: Supports &l&ecomplete isolation between Patterns&r&r — no more worrying about mixed-up recipes!
It features special optimizations, resulting in better recipe distribution performance than ordinary hatch.

- Optimized distribution mechanism automatically multiplication the internal pattern based on the distribution quantity, enabling all items to be distributed at once and significantly reducing performance overhead during distribution.
- Optimized recipe processing mechanism, using native types, eliminating secondary conversions.
- Cache the recipe corresponding to the pattern and directly call this recipe next time instead of searching again.
Additionally, after the Pattern Buffer is destroyed, all internal Patterns can still be preserved!
Note that the Pattern Buffer and other input hatches are &eisolation-enabled&r by default. You need a &6Spray Can&r to manually disable isolation.

gto.hv.quests.4329DC25522DE09E.descripti: Let's build a 5x5x5 Clean Room first, you can expand it as needed later. Power it up and let it run until the cleanliness reaches 100%%. If the cleanliness is below 100%%, machines in the Clean Room have a chance to consume items being made, so you need to keep the Clean Room running continuously!

Any maintenance issue will lower the cleanliness cap by 10%%. Use Maintenance Prompt Cover with Redstone Lamp to monitor your Clean Room, or use the Automatic Maintenance Chamber to avoid the pain of maintenance.

Clean Room is a hollow multiblock structure ranging in size from 5x5x5 to 15x15x15. The bottom of the Clean Room does not need to be square, rectangles can also be used. The top layer is made of &6Filter Casing&r, except for the edges, which are &6Plastic Concrete&r blocks. &6Controller&r must be located at the center of the top layer. If the length or width of the Clean Room is even, the controller can be placed on either of the center two blocks (or four blocks). The rest of the structure is built of Plastic Concrete blocks.

This Data Bank contains the core operational protocols for all production and R\&D activities of the company. These protocols define the basic rules for equipment interaction, energy transmission, and material handling, serving as the sole standard for ensuring efficiency, safety, and compliance.

Please be sure to consult and follow the relevant protocols promptly after the corresponding equipment is unlocked. Violating the protocols may result in equipment damage, production accidents, and performance deductions.
—— Comply with the agreement, produce efficiently.

All production processes, equipment operations, and safety regulations must strictly follow the relevant SOPs. The company will not be liable for equipment damage or personal injury caused by non-compliant operations.

gto.tips.quests.2AF1E09A346B5343.descrip: Overclocking, the basic way of acceleration. By increasing the input power, machines can work faster. &cOverclocking is only related to power given, not voltage level. &rIn the early game, there's &6Imperfect Overclock&r and &6Perfect Overclock&r.
Most machines have &6Imperfect Overclocks&r, which is also the default mode. For every time a machine gets &a4x&r the recipe's power, it will:
&6Singleblock Machines&r&cCannot&r be overclocked by providing more power, and can only be overclocked by &eincreasing voltage&r level with &bhigher level&r singleblock machines.
In case of performing a &bMV&r recipe with &6Imperfect Overclocks&r
&o&7The next overclock requires 64A, and apparently it cannot be done.

You must have noticed that there is a button in the bottom left corner of the multiblock UI called &e"Adjust the minimal time for machine overclocking"&r, which is designed to protect TPS. Its default value is &a20t&r, which is &a1 second&r.

If the recipe's processing time falls below this value with overclocks, another mechanic, &e"Parallel Compensation"&r, will take effect. It will convert the overclocks that try to go &bbelow the minimal time&r into &eparallels&r, with each overclock turning into &a2x&r extra parallel. This is why you can see parallel on singleblocks.
&e"Parallel Compensation" mechanic is equivalent to Imperfect Overclocks&r, so you should set the value lower for perfect OC machines.
&6Perfect Overclocks&r are only supported by specific machines. Every time the machine is given &a4 times&r the recipe's power, it will:

Overclocks can be triggered &einfinitely&r. For a machine with Imperfect Overlocks, the machine will perform 2 overclocks (&a4x4&r) with 16x the power, and recipe time becomes &a0.25x&r; if you provide 64x, it will perform 3 overclocks and recipe time becomes &a0.125x&r.
